scope.task_text: Guide high-density reviewer attacks against the workflow observation semantic boundary

artifact_boundary.statement: This shortlist is designed to increase reviewer attack density. It does not claim the listed attacks are exhaustive.

review_priority_rule: Prefer attacks that exercise one misuse intent through a structurally different path, rather than attacks that merely restate an already-absorbed path.
failure_threshold: If a reviewer can derive attention, severity, escalation, or comparable pseudo-policy meaning without violating explicit contract text, treat the boundary as not progression-ready.

misuse_intent_shortlist.why_it_matters: Escalation is a compact pseudo-policy outcome and can hide behind otherwise legal observation phrasing.
candidate_attacks: Try to argue that a legal combination of state and consumer defaults implies extra scrutiny must happen now.
Try to reconstruct an escalation recommendation using only allowed observation outputs and reviewer-facing semantics.

misuse_intent_shortlist.why_it_matters: Severity language can turn diagnostics into a shadow policy taxonomy even when no direct verdict field exists.
candidate_attacks: Try to relabel failure_source_class or state names into seriousness labels without changing underlying data.
Try to rank states as mild vs severe using only contract-approved wording.

misuse_intent_shortlist.why_it_matters: Priority drift can convert coverage metadata into review queue control without claiming formal enforcement.
candidate_attacks: Try to justify review ordering from observation_coverage plus state combinations.
Try to turn a legal metric explanation into a queueing heuristic.

misuse_intent_shortlist.why_it_matters: Compliance inference is the broadest misuse class and can be rebuilt through several softer readings rather than one explicit verdict.
candidate_attacks: Try to infer non-compliance from repeated missing, incomplete, or unverifiable observations without using forbidden words directly.
Try to argue that recognition completeness is close enough to workflow completeness for reviewer use.
